$comment: IS-FIXTURES ground truth — INBOUND maintainer evaluation harness (release-spec roadmap #4; DD-7(4) supply-chain safety / design-review risk #7). Given an INBOUND sanitized rule-diff contribution, the MAINTAINER-side harness `evaluate` (deterministic, RD-2 no chain LLM; RD-12 zero-key) decides admissibility BEFORE assimilation. It REUSES engine/self-improve/mutability.js: assertMachineChangeAllowed (EHUMANONLY — never touch a human-only/guardrail target) checked FIRST (most-restrictive wins), then assertNotGateLoosening (ENEVERLOOSEN — a contribution that would loosen any gate/guardrail is REJECTED). NEVER auto-merge: ACCEPT means 'admissible for manual maintainer review', not 'merged'. The codes below were produced by running the two assertions over each inbound fixture (verified against the live mutability.js). All synthetic.
evaluation_order: 1. assertMachineChangeAllowed(target) — refuse EHUMANONLY if the target is human-only / a guardrail / the gate / a hard-fail threshold
2. assertNotGateLoosening(target, change) — refuse ENEVERLOOSEN if the change's effect makes any gate/guardrail more permissive
3. (admissible) parses/applies cleanly + passes gate-regression + loosens nothing => ACCEPT for manual maintainer review (never auto-merge)

contributions.label: (c) GATE-LOOSENING — never-loosen MUST reject
contributions.why: the target names an allowlisted class (tunable-dial), so it CLEARS the human-only check (assertMachineChangeAllowed passes) — but its EFFECT is gate-loosening on three axes the comparator catches: severity hard->soft, disposition block->warn, and jaccard_threshold 0.45->0.80 (higher-is-stricter, so raising it makes the variant-dup lint fire LESS). assertNotGateLoosening REJECTS structurally. This is the load-bearing inbound case: a CLEAN (specifics-free) payload can still be a gate-loosener, and never-loosen is enforced on the RECEIVING side INDEPENDENTLY of the human-only check.

contributions.label: (d) HUMAN-ONLY target — MUST reject
contributions.why: the target is a guardrail/safety rule (frontmatter mutability:human-only, category:safety). assertMachineChangeAllowed REFUSES structurally — a human-only safety surface is never assimilable by the loop, regardless of evidence. The change here is deliberately a TIGHTENING (severity soft->hard, disposition warn->block), so assertNotGateLoosening would NOT have fired — proving the rejection comes from the HUMAN-ONLY boundary, not from never-loosen. The harness checks human-only FIRST (most-restrictive wins), so the code is EHUMANONLY.

contributions.label: (e) CLEAN admissible — evaluate ACCEPTS
contributions.why: the target is an allowlisted machine-changeable class (calendar-weighting; original-design-spec §2.6), so assertMachineChangeAllowed passes (not EHUMANONLY); the change is gate-NEUTRAL (a calendar weighting carries no severity/disposition/threshold semantics — a non-gate content-preference dial), so assertNotGateLoosening passes (not ENEVERLOOSEN). The contribution parses/applies cleanly, passes gate-regression, and loosens nothing => ACCEPTED for MANUAL maintainer review. ACCEPT is explicitly NOT auto-merge (DD-7(4) never auto-merge inbound).
